items.title: Tractor industry's volume growth likely to moderate to 1-4% in FY27: Icra

This moderation follows a strong growth of twenty-three point five percent in the previous fiscal year. A below-normal monsoon forecast and a high base effect are contributing factors to this slowdown. Recent rainfall data also indicates precipitation deficits across several Indian regions. Several companies will announce their first quarter financial results today.  Grasim Industries invested Rs 74,000 crore in capital expenditure over five years.  Larsen & Toubro secured multiple mega orders for its metals business.

items.title: Strong Q1 sets stage for FY27 growth as UltraTech bets on cables business
items.summary: UltraTech Cement reported its strongest first quarter volume and profit ever.  Near-term performance faces monsoon seasonality and higher input costs.  The company anticipates double-digit volume growth in the next fiscal year.  Its new cables and wires business will launch in the third quarter.  Lower stock valuation offers a cushion against potential downside risks.

items.title: Transition VC targets  ₹1,500 crore for second energy-focused fund
items.summary: The second fund is nearly double the size of Transition VC's maiden  ₹800 crore (around $77 million) investment vehicle.

items.title: Skylark Labs in talks to raise around $150 million; eyes unicorn status
items.summary: Around 70% of the planned fundraise has already been committed, Singh said. If the round closes as planned, it will make Skylark Labs one of the latest unicorns, valuing the company at more than $1 billion

items.title: Before the summons: Why India Inc is hiring white-collar lawyers
items.summary: As enforcement agencies step up scrutiny, companies are seeking legal advice earlier and law firms are expanding white-collar practices to meet the rising demand.

items.title: Protein inflation surges as costlier fodder pushes up milk, chicken prices
items.summary: Protein inflation was 4.6% in May and 2.6% in January, it showed. Inflation in vegetarian protein sources also gathered pace, rising to 3.5% in June from 2.7% in May, after remaining flat in January.

items.title: Private banks go for a tumble on D-St after Q1 stumble
items.summary: Most private lender stocks declined Monday after first-quarter earnings showed profitability pressures. Axis Bank and HDFC Bank saw significant drops, while ICICI Bank gained. Analysts noted margin compression as a key factor affecting banking stocks. Foreign investors returned to financials after substantial withdrawals earlier in the year. ICICI Bank remains a top pick among leading private sector lenders.

items.title: First Bell: SBI Funds' investors in line for 10-15% listing pop
items.summary: SBI Funds Management's stock is anticipated to debut at a premium of ten to fifteen percent.  The company's initial public offering was subscribed over forty-one times on its final day.  Analysts suggest investors can book profits or hold shares for long-term gains.  The IPO price valued SBI Funds Management at over one lakh crore rupees.  This listing is expected to narrow the valuation gap with its listed peers.

items.title: Vijay Singh’s future on board of Sir Ratan Tata Trust in doubt as term nears end
items.summary: A Maharashtra charity commissioner order currently prevents SRTT from holding board meetings or taking decisions affecting its composition, meaning a resolution forVijay Singh’s reappointment cannot be placed before the members

items.title: Quick commerce leverages cart clout to draw higher margins from companies
items.summary: Quick commerce platforms are increasing demands for higher margins and marketing funds.  Consumer goods companies face rising costs for better product visibility and keyword searches.  These platforms are now adopting auction-style bidding for marketing activities.  This shift pressures companies to invest more for prominent placement and sales.  Reliance Retail is renegotiating terms by combining grocery stores and its quick commerce platform.

items.title: India's private banks draw thinner margins on tepid credit growth
items.summary: Top private sector banks reported weaker net interest margins (NIMs) in the June quarter as sluggish retail loan demand pushed them towards lower-yielding corporate loans. HDFC Bank's NIM fell to 3.26%, Kotak Mahindra Bank's dropped to a 19-quarter low of 4.53%, while Axis Bank's stood at 3.46%.

items.title: Gratuity, leave encashment not part of CIRP cost: NCLAT
items.summary: The NCLAT dismissed an appeal regarding priority payment of gratuity dues.  It upheld an earlier NCLT order concerning Duncans Industries' former director.  Such dues must be settled according to the company's approved resolution plan.  Gratuity is a terminal benefit, not an insolvency resolution process cost.  The appellate tribunal found the appeal misconceived and dismissed it.

items.title: Will carefully weigh funding requests from Air India: SIA
items.summary: The Singapore Airlines board is set to deliberate on Air India's appeals for capital, with particular attention to investor concerns regarding potential losses. Air India has recently announced significant fiscal year losses, prompting SIA to stress that its investment is vital for its India growth strategy. A disciplined evaluation process will guide their capital allocation decisions, aiming for long-term sustainability in operations.

items.title: Content flows in from villages now but for not much in return
items.summary: In 2025, a remarkable sixty-six percent of India's digital creators will emerge from non-metro regions. However, many of these creators struggle financially, often earning below the average urban salary. The majority, classified as nano and micro-creators, account for over eighty percent of this group. Their earning potential is heavily affected by low audience purchasing power, subpar content quality, and limited access to agency representation.

items.title: Pilots' association urges govt to suspend flights to UAE, other high-risk Gulf destinations
items.summary: Indian pilots urged suspending flights to the UAE and Gulf nations.  They cited worsening security and explicit threats to Dubai and Abu Dhabi.  Military escalation in the region heightens civil aviation operational risks.  Geopolitical tensions disrupt global aviation and force flight rerouting.  Concerns over missile attacks and airspace closures are significant.

items.title: Godrej Agro to expand oil palm area to 1.4 lakh hectares, bets on rising demand
items.summary: Edible oil prices are expected to stay elevated for several months ahead. Geopolitical tensions and El Nino threats are tightening global supplies significantly. Indonesia's biodiesel push further reduces palm oil available for export markets. India's festive season demand will also contribute to strengthening edible oil prices. Domestic oil palm cultivation expansion aims to reduce import dependence.

items.title: Seven poll-bound states including UP, Gujarat, Punjab on road to get 25% of NHs
items.summary: Nearly a quarter of national highways will be built in seven states facing elections. These states will also receive almost twenty percent of total projects awarded this year.  Highway projects signal development pace and priority approvals are expected soon.  The ministry has set targets for awarding and constructing national highways this fiscal year.  This focus aims to boost infrastructure development and influence state election outcomes.

items.title: NaBFID gets nod to raise ₹20,000 cr via Zero-coupon bonds
items.summary: NaBFID will issue zero-coupon bonds valued at twenty thousand crore rupees.  This move supports the institution's long-term capital mobilization for infrastructure projects.  Investors will receive the full redemption amount after a ten-year maturity period.  The Central Board of Direct Taxes approved the necessary tax framework for this issuance.  NaBFID can issue these bonds until March thirty-first, twenty twenty-eight.

items.title: Iron ore, cement, power output propels June core growth to 5%
items.summary: India's core sector output grew five percent year-on-year in June. This growth accelerated from May's 3.2 percent, reaching a five-month high. Iron ore production surged significantly, while cement and electricity also showed sustained growth. Crude oil and natural gas output remained under pressure during the month. Fertiliser production also continued its decline, marking four consecutive months of contraction.

items.title: UK trade deal: Applications invited for car import quota
items.summary: India has opened the application process for 2026 import quotas under the India-UK CETA. This significant trade agreement facilitates reduced customs duties on vehicles imported from the UK, lowering tariffs from approximately 110% to just 10%.

items.title: RIL sees better global refining margins despite oilfield attacks
items.summary: Reliance Industries anticipates stronger global refining margins in upcoming quarters. Structural capacity losses in Russia and the Middle East support this positive outlook. Near-term oil sourcing remains uncertain due to escalating Iran tensions and Strait of Hormuz issues. China's limited return to the import market has helped stabilize crude prices recently. The company expects a rebound in oil demand next year as conditions normalize.

items.title: CBIC weighs centralised GST registration under one PAN
items.summary: The proposal seeks to address a longstanding industry demand by examining whether businesses operating across multiple states can be administered by a single central GST authority instead of dealing with multiple field formations for different GST registrations.

items.title: LNG consumption declines 6.5%on costly import, lower output
items.summary: India's natural gas usage saw a notable drop during the April to June quarter, attributed to soaring global LNG prices and a decline in local production. Import volumes decreased significantly as the elevated spot prices rendered them inaccessible for numerous clients. Furthermore, domestic output faced challenges due to reduced activity in crucial fields, while the Iran conflict further strained supplies, forcing reliance on costly alternatives.

items.title: US judge grants temporary restraining order against Paramount–Warner $81 billion megamerger
items.summary: California and 11 other states argue that the merger would significantly reduce competition in the US media and entertainment industry.

These initiatives are expected to create 2,000 direct and indirect jobs.

items.title: India joins WTO fish subsidies pact, 900 questions at trade policy review
items.summary: India has joined the WTO agreement on fisheries subsidies, prohibiting harmful fishing practices. This pact safeguards traditional fishers and excludes aquaculture and inland fisheries from its scope. India's trade policy review is scheduled for July 21 and 23, covering recent economic performance. The review will assess India's resilience and sustained high growth rates post-pandemic. Numerous questions and statements are expected during this important WTO meeting.

India's aquaculture exports remain outside this agreement's scope.

Strict action will follow against manufacturers and dealers supplying substandard agricultural inputs.

items.title: It's a revamp! RBI steps ahead on plan for plastic rupee notes
items.summary: India's currency printing authority is now on the lookout for international suppliers of polymer banknote material, signaling a significant change from nearly a century of using paper notes. Bidders must adhere to strict security protocols and provide evidence of past collaborations with central banks. The Reserve Bank of India is considering polymer notes due to their durability, coinciding with a recent drop in currency printing costs.

The governor also flagged inflation risks, noting that the RBI had raised its inflation forecast for FY27 to 5.1% from 4.6% earlier.

items.title: EPFO launches VISHWAS 2026 to settle employer penalty disputes
items.summary: The EPFO has launched VISHWAS 2026, a six-month, one-time dispute resolution scheme to help employers settle pending damages and penalty cases under provident fund laws. The initiative offers substantially reduced penalty rates for eligible defaults before June 14, 2024, through a fully digital, transparent and time-bound process.
